activities_projects.title: In-Depth Character Analysis of Surang
activities_projects.objective: Examine the psychological and moral aspects of the main character
steps: Read 'The Tunnel' carefully and note Surang’s key actions and decisions
Identify traits that reflect courage, curiosity, fear, and moral judgement
Analyse how Surang’s behaviour changes in different situations
Support your analysis with textual evidence and quotations
Prepare a detailed report and present it in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn critical thinking and advanced literary analysis
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis, textual support, and clarity of explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Symbolism and Theme Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Interpret symbols and underlying messages in the story
steps: Identify symbols such as the tunnel, darkness, and the journey
Discuss what each symbol represents in terms of human experience
Relate the symbols to themes like curiosity, courage, and self-discovery
Write an analytical paragraph explaining your interpretation
Present your findings in class discussion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop interpretive skills and thematic understanding
activities_projects.evaluation: Originality of interpretation and textual justification (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Analytical Story Mapping
activities_projects.objective: Understand narrative structure and cause-effect relationships
steps: Divide the story into key events
Create a cause-effect diagram showing how each event influences the next
Highlight turning points and climax of the story
Include character reactions and consequences in the map
Present the analytical map to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop advanced comprehension and analytical skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of cause-effect relationships and clarity of presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Critical Dialogue Writing
activities_projects.objective: Recreate and enhance dialogue with analysis of tone and intention
steps: Select a significant dialogue from the story
Rewrite it emphasizing tone, emotion, and hidden meanings
Include analytical notes explaining character motives and language choices
Read your rewritten dialogue and analysis to classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve critical writing and interpretation of literary dialogue
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy, depth of analysis, and presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Role Play with Analytical Commentary
steps: Form groups and select a key scene from the story
Perform the scene with expressions and gestures
After performance, provide a commentary analyzing characters’ decisions and underlying themes
Answer questions from classmates about your interpretation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop performance, analytical, and oral communication skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Performance quality and depth of analytical commentary (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Advanced Vocabulary and Contextual Usage
activities_projects.objective: Enhance understanding of nuanced words and expressions
steps: Identify at least 15 challenging words from the story
Find multiple meanings and context-specific usage
Use each word in two different sentences reflecting different contexts
Explain your choice of context to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve advanced vocabulary and contextual understanding
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy, creativity, and contextual appropriateness (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sensory and Emotional Descriptive Writing
activities_projects.objective: Develop detailed descriptive writing using emotional depth
steps: Imagine being inside the tunnel with Surang
Write a detailed descriptive passage including sensory details and emotional reactions
Use figurative language and literary devices to enhance imagery
Read your passage and receive peer feedback for improvement
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students enhance descriptive and creative writing skills with literary sophistication
activities_projects.evaluation: Use of literary devices, sensory details, and emotional depth (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Word Formation and Etymology Study
activities_projects.objective: Explore word origins and advanced formation techniques
steps: Select complex words from the story
Break each word into root, prefix, and suffix
Research the etymology and historical origin
Write a short paragraph explaining the word's development and meaning
Share your research with the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students gain advanced vocabulary skills and understanding of language evolution
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of analysis, depth of research, and clarity of explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Complex Sentence and Punctuation Exercise
activities_projects.objective: Master complex sentence structures and punctuation nuances
steps: Identify complex and compound-complex sentences from the story
Analyze the use of punctuation for effect
Rewrite three sentences using advanced punctuation and varied clauses
Explain the impact of punctuation on tone and meaning
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students enhance grammar, syntax, and stylistic writing skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Correctness, stylistic effectiveness, and explanation clarity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Critical Alternate Ending
activities_projects.objective: Exercise creativity while maintaining narrative coherence and depth
steps: Analyse the story’s ending and character motivations
Develop an alternate ending that maintains logical consistency and thematic relevance
Include reflections on characters’ thoughts and emotional responses
Present your alternate ending with a brief analytical commentary
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop high-level creative writing and critical thinking
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, coherence, thematic relevance, and analytical depth (10 marks)
